zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Brenchley Moore於1474年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 盧斯盧斯時，他的父親Thomas William Moore36歲，母親Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden24歲。他於大約1499年與Catherine Wrath Aucher在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1504年在英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登享年30歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Brenchley Moore于1474年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 盧斯盧斯时，他的父亲Thomas William Moore36岁，母亲Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden24岁。他于大约1499年与Catherine Wrath Aucher在英国结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1504年在英国英格兰肯特貝嫩登享年30岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特貝嫩登。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: Brenchley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from a place in Kent named Brenchley, from an Old English personal name Brænci (of uncertain origin) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Brenchley Moore was born in 1474, in Louth, County Louth, Ireland, his father, Thomas William Moore, was 36 and his mother, Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden, was 24. He married Catherine Wrath Aucher about 1499, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1504, in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 30, and was buried in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Brenchley Moore est né en 1474, à Louth, County Louth, Irlande, son père, Thomas William Moore, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Wrath Aucher environ 1499, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1504, à Benenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Benenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Brenchley Moore 1474 in Louth, County Louth, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas William Moore 36 und seine Mutter Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden 24. Er hat um 1499 in England Catherine Wrath Aucher geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1504 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Catherine Wrath Aucher circa 1499, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1504, nel Benenden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 30 anni, e fu sepolto a Benenden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Brenchley Moore(이)가 1474년 아일랜드 카운티 루스 루스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas William Moore(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1499년 영국에서 Catherine Wrath Aucher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1504년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베니든에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베니든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Brenchley Moore родился в 1474 г. в местности лоут, коунти лоут, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Thomas William Moore было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Catherine Benchley Benenden, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Wrath Aucher около 1499 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1504 г. в местности биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
